title: Reminiscing, praying, and caring
sub_topics: 1957 health fee opposition as FEU CSO president
ruptured appendix (FEU Hospital, Dr. Ricardo Alfonso)
1949 jeepney accident — army truck rolling over him
Fr. Michael Nolan teaching basic prayers
Our Father / Hail Mary / Glory Be / FEU Prayer
Filipino-style medical care anecdote (multibillionaire in US hospital)

stances.claim: Our Lord Jesus and His mother Mary were responsible for all his miraculous escapes from death — dysentery in 1942, ruptured appendix in 1957, jeepney accident in 1949, diabetes since 1983, heart-rhythm skips — and 'whatever I may have achieved in my humble life and career' must be ascribed to Christ's ever-presence.

First advice: Always pray and trust God — evils, devils, defeats, difficulties, and obstacles will always be present; the prayer should not be for God to banish them but for the strength to triumph over them; God helps 'in His own way, in His own time, not in our way, not in our time.'

Second advice: Work with two Cs — competence and care; medical professionals must treat patients as human beings with emotions of fear, loneliness, and desperation, not merely as objects to be studied, operated on, and monitored.
stances.rhetorical_move: two-C framework + cautionary anecdote about US hospital

stances.claim: The summary maxim: 'Pray as though everything depended on God; work as though everything depended on you.'

institutions: Far Eastern University-Nicanor Reyes Medical Foundation (FEU-NRMF)

signature_phrases: Pray as though everything depended on God; work as though everything depended on you
Our Father, the Hail Mary, the Glory Be, and the FEU Prayer
in His own way, in His own time, not in our way, not in our time
ever present during my illnesses and healings, my trials and triumphs, my defeats and victories
register_markers: THOUGH MERELY A SOPHOMORE LAW STUDENT IN 1957
decision_framework_signals: applies providential autobiography lens — medical escapes from death as evidence of Christ's presence
uses 'pray for strength, not for relief' as paradoxical-providence framework
applies two-Cs framework (competence + care) as medical-professional ethic
uses care anecdote to argue distinctively Filipino model of family-presence in medical care
notable_anecdotes: Sophomore CSO president opposing the P6 health and hospitalization fee, then being rushed to the FEU Hospital with white blood count over 36,000 and a ruptured appendix at the rear left of his abdomen — Dr. Alfonso asking 'Do you want to pay the full hospital bills or the six-peso fee?' and CJ softly conceding 'Doctor, you win'
Being 'lucky' that his appendix had ruptured two weeks earlier but fat enveloped the poison
Surviving dysentery at age 6 in 1942 with no hospital/medicine/doctor/nurse, recovering in his mother's arms after being 'given up for dead'
Diabetes diagnosis in 1983, controlled with help from Dr. Litonjua whom he still sees every three months
Three operations for recurring hemorrhoids; failing ECG, 2D echo, and treadmill stress tests but passing angiogram — Dr. Morales explaining a rare natural heart-rhythm skip
1949 jeepney-jumping accident: an oncoming 6x6 army truck rolling over his lean thin body which slid under its axles
Fr. Michael Nolan as FEU chaplain teaching him the Our Father, Hail Mary, Glory Be, and FEU Prayer — the FEU Prayer becoming a source for the 2001 Centennial Prayer of the Courts

Filipino multibillionaire (now deceased) confined in a top US hospital for heart bypass and kidney transplant, treated 'merely as an object to be studied, operated on, and monitored' — longing for Filipino-style family presence and care
